compile.description: Compiles a C/C++ source file into an executable
commands: gcc source.c  # compiles to a.out (default)
gcc source.c -o my_program  # compile with custom name [citation:1]
gcc source1.c source2.c -o program  # compile multiple files [citation:1]
g++ source.cpp -o my_program  # compile C++ with g++ [citation:5]
gcc source.c -o program && ./program  # compile and run

compile-only.description: Compiles to object file without linking (for multi-file projects)
commands: gcc -c source.c  # generates source.o [citation:1]
gcc -c source1.c source2.c  # compile multiple without linking [citation:1]
gcc -c -o custom.o source.c  # specify object filename
g++ -c source.cpp -o source.o  # C++ object file [citation:5]

link.description: Links object files into an executable
commands: gcc file1.o file2.o -o program  # link object files [citation:1]
gcc *.o -o program  # link all object files
g++ file1.o file2.o -o program  # C++ linking

debug.description: Compiles with debugging symbols for GDB
commands: gcc -g source.c -o program  # add debug symbols [citation:2]
gcc -ggdb source.c -o program  # GDB-specific debugging [citation:12]
gcc -g -O0 source.c -o program  # debug with no optimization [citation:2]
g++ -g -std=c++17 source.cpp -o program  # C++ with debug [citation:5]

warnings.description: Compiles with warning flags to catch potential issues
commands: gcc -Wall source.c -o program  # all standard warnings [citation:2]
gcc -Wall -Wextra source.c -o program  # extra warnings [citation:5]
gcc -Wall -Wextra -pedantic source.c -o program  # strict ISO C [citation:2]
gcc -Wall -Werror source.c -o program  # treat warnings as errors [citation:5]
gcc -Wall -Wextra -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion source.c -o program  # comprehensive warnings [citation:5]
gcc -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -Werror -Wshadow -Wsign-conversion source.c -o program  # production ready [citation:5]
gcc -O0 source.c -o program  # no optimization (default)
gcc -O1 source.c -o program  # basic optimization
gcc -O2 source.c -o program  # good optimization [citation:5]
gcc -O3 source.c -o program  # aggressive optimization (may increase size)
gcc -O2 -Wall source.c -o program  # optimize with warnings [citation:5]
gcc -Os source.c -o program  # optimize for size

include.description: Adds directories to search for header files
commands: gcc -I /path/to/include source.c -o program  # add include path [citation:5]
gcc -I./libs -I/usr/local/include source.c -o program  # multiple paths
gcc -I$(pwd)/include source.c -o program  # use current directory
gcc -I /path/to/include -Wall source.c -o program  # with warnings
gcc source.c -lm -o program  # link math library [citation:1]
gcc source.c -lpthread -o program  # link pthreads
gcc -L /path/to/lib -lfoo source.c -o program  # specify lib path and name [citation:1]
gcc source.c -static -lmylib -o program  # force static linking [citation:1]
gcc source.c -shared -fPIC -o libmylib.so  # create shared library [citation:7]
gcc source.c -lfoo -o program  # link libfoo.a or libfoo.so

std.description: Specifies the C/C++ language standard to use
commands: gcc -std=c99 source.c -o program  # C99 standard [citation:3]
gcc -std=c11 source.c -o program  # C11 standard
gcc -std=c17 source.c -o program  # C17 standard [citation:3]
g++ -std=c++11 source.cpp -o program  # C++11 [citation:5]
g++ -std=c++14 source.cpp -o program  # C++14
g++ -std=c++17 source.cpp -o program  # C++17 [citation:5]
g++ -std=c++20 source.cpp -o program  # C++20
gcc -ansi source.c -o program  # ANSI/ISO C standard [citation:7]

preprocess.description: Runs only the preprocessor to expand macros and includes
commands: gcc -E source.c  # output preprocessed code [citation:7]
gcc -E source.c -o source.i  # save preprocessed output [citation:12]
gcc -E -DDEBUG source.c  # with macro definitions
gcc -S source.c  # generates source.s [citation:12]
gcc -S -O2 source.c -o optimized.s  # optimized assembly
gcc -S -masm=intel source.c  # Intel syntax assembly
gcc -S -fverbose-asm source.c  # verbose assembly comments

define.description: Defines a macro at compile time
commands: gcc -DDEBUG source.c -o program  # define DEBUG macro [citation:7]
gcc -DVERSION=\"1.0\" source.c -o program  # define with value
gcc -D__DEBUG__ -Wall source.c -o program  # common debug macro
gcc -D_GNU_SOURCE source.c -o program  # GNU extensions

profiling.description: Compiles with profiling information for gprof
commands: gcc -pg source.c -o program  # compile for profiling [citation:3]
gcc -pg -O2 source.c -o program  # profile with optimization
./program && gprof program gmon.out > analysis.txt  # generate report

optimize-warn.description: Compiles with optimization and full warnings (production ready)
commands: gcc -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic source.c -o program  # safe production build [citation:5]
gcc -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion source.c -o program  # comprehensive
g++ -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -std=c++17 source.cpp -o program  # C++ production [citation:5]
gcc -fprofile-generate source.c -o program && ./program  # generate profile data
gcc -fprofile-use source.c -o program  # use profile data [citation:3]
gcc -fprofile-generate -fprofile-use source.c -o program  # combined
gcc -shared -fPIC source.c -o libmylib.so  # create shared library [citation:7]
gcc -shared -fPIC -Wl,-soname,libmylib.so.1 -o libmylib.so.1.0 file1.o file2.o  # versioned lib
gcc -shared -fPIC -O2 source.c -o libmylib.so  # optimized shared lib
gcc -v source.c -o program  # show compiler details [citation:3]
gcc -### source.c  # show commands but don't run [citation:3]
gcc -v -O2 source.c -o program  # verbose with optimization
gcc -### -Wall source.c  # show what would happen
arm-linux-gnueabihf-gcc source.c -o program  # compile for ARM [citation:3]
arm-linux-gnueabihf-gcc -static source.c -o program  # static ARM build
arm-linux-gnueabihf-g++ source.cpp -o program  # C++ for ARM
gcc -dumpversion  # show GCC version [citation:8]
gcc -dumpmachine  # show target machine [citation:8]
gcc -dumpspecs  # show compiler specs [citation:8]
gcc --help=warnings  # list all warning options [citation:5]
